project.project_name: Modern-Era Retrospective analysis for Research and Applications Version 2 (MERRA-2)

resource.resource_name: US National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) Global Modeling and Assimilation Office (GMAO) Modern-Era Retrospective analysis for Research and Applications Version 2 (MERRA-2) tavgM_2d_chm_Nx: 2-dimensional, Monthly mean, Time-Averaged, Single-Level, Assimilation, Carbon Monoxide and Ozone Diagnostics (M2TMNXCHM), Version 5.12.4
resource.resource_description: A monthly time-averaged 2-dimensional data collection of atmospheric carbon monoxide and ozone parameters is presented in the Modern-Era Retrospective analysis for Research and Applications version 2 (MERRA-2). It provides global model and data assimilation results that incorporate satellite-based observations to predict monthly carbon monoxide and ozone parameters, such as properties of carbon monoxide (column burden, emission, chemical production, and surface concentration), and total column ozone.

publication.citation: Global Modeling and Assimilation Office (GMAO) (2015), MERRA-2 tavgM_2d_chm_Nx: 2d, Monthly mean, Time-Averaged, Single-Level, Assimilation, Carbon Monoxide and Ozone Diagnostics V5.12.4, Greenbelt, MD, USA, Goddard Earth Sciences Data and Information Services Center (GES DISC)

Gelaro, R., McCarty, W., Suárez, M. J., Todling, R., Molod, A., Takacs, L., ... & Zhao, B. (2017). The modern-era retrospective analysis for research and applications, version 2 (MERRA-2). Journal of climate, 30(14), 5419-5454.

key_variables: Surface-level concentration and column burden of carbon monoxide
example_metrics: Surface-level air pollutant concentration estimates for grid cell containing geocoded address

intended_use.value: Geospatial exposure assessment for public health studies on shorter-term or longer-term outcomes
Geospatial exposure assessment at the individual-level or population-level

strengths: Dataset supports analysis of monthly differences in exposure to air pollution over multiple decades across global locations
limitations: Dataset cannot be used to analyze patterns of spatial variability within ~50 km grid resolution grid cells (i.e., within the same city in some cases)
Dataset cannot be used to analyze patterns of spatial variability within ~50 km grid resolution grid cells (i.e., within the same city in some cases)
